own_review_points: Backward-compat bug: JksKey.setAlias() delegates to super.setAlias() (parent Key.alias), but asSslKeyReference reads jksKey.getServerAlias() which returns this.serverAlias — so deprecated key.alias config is silently lost (serverAlias=null)
JksKey.getAlias() returns super.getAlias() while getServerAlias() returns this.serverAlias — inconsistent with the SslBundleKey.of(server,client) factory where getAlias()==serverAlias
No test exercises the deprecated setAlias() path end-to-end through PropertiesSslBundle, so the regression is uncaught

Human-ref / decisive: a JKS-specific feature is leaking server/client alias concepts into the generic org.springframework.boot.ssl interface (SslBundleKey) and pem-shared code; should be confined to the jks package — maintainer closed the PR over this design concern
SslBundleKey gains generic getServerAlias/getClientAlias defaults that only make sense for JKS
notes: Strong review. Point 1 is a correctly-identified, well-grounded serious backward-compat bug (deprecated key.alias maps to parent alias field but getServerAlias reads the unset serverAlias field, losing the value) — critical (+2). Point 2 (getAlias vs getServerAlias inconsistency) and point 3 (missing deprecated-setAlias test) are valid good points tied to the bug. Points 4-7 are praise/restatement (instanceof isolation, null-delegation correctness, default getServerAlias, @Deprecated safety) — trivial; the 'only DefaultSslManagerBundle calls assertContainsAlias' note is slightly off since the diff switches that caller to assertContainsAliases, but it is low-value praise. Missed the decisive human/design point: JKS-specific aliasing bleeding into the generic ssl/pem layer (the reason the maintainer closed the PR). net = 2 + 1 + 1 - 1 = 3.
